panels.description: Medium shot: The Narrator is sweating and covered in grime, digging a narrow tunnel in a dark cellar. A single candle flickers nearby, casting long, wavering shadows. His expression is one of deep doubt and exhaustion. In the background, the artilleryman watches him silently.

bubbles.text: Doubts began to arise... why dig this long tunnel when we could enter the drain directly?

panels.description: Medium shot: The artilleryman drops his spade with a heavy thud. He wipes his brow with a dirty sleeve, looking up toward the ceiling. The lighting is harsh from below.

bubbles.text: Let us knock off a bit. I think it's time we reconnoitred from the roof of the house.

panels.description: Close-up: A split-focus panel. The Narrator looks at the artilleryman with sharp, sudden suspicion. The artilleryman responds with a nonchalant, slightly lazy expression, leaning against the dirt wall.

bubbles.text: Why were you walking about the common instead of being here?
Taking the air. I was coming back. It's safer by night.

panels.description: Wide shot: The two men are on the roof, crouching low behind a stone parapet. They are peering out over the edge. The Narrator looks tense, while the artilleryman points toward the horizon. The sky is a pale, eerie blue.

bubbles.text: We ought to reconnoitre now, before they hear the spades and drop upon us unawares.

panels.description: Wide shot: A panoramic view from the roof. The landscape is dominated by alien red weed that chokes the river and crawls up the trees of the old palace. In the distance, thick black smoke and blue haze obscure the London skyline. The sunlight is bright but the scene is desolate.

bubbles.text: The river was a bubbly mass of red weed... the branches gaunt and dead amid the clusters.

panels.description: Action shot: A dramatic flashback image. A giant Martian fighting machine looms over Regent Street. Below its towering metallic legs, a crowd of ragged, drunken figures dance in the artificial glow of electric lights, oblivious to the metal horror looking down at them. High contrast, cinematic lighting.

bubbles.text: A group of fools had the electric light in order... and as day came they saw a fighting-machine looking down at them.
He picked up nearly a hundred too drunk or frightened to run.

panels.description: Medium close shot: The Narrator stares at the artilleryman, his eyes showing a mix of renewed belief and grim understanding. The artilleryman is speaking with conviction, his silhouette framed against the smoke-filled horizon.

bubbles.text: I began to believe in him again... and the importance of doing nothing precipitately.
